vulnerability.title: Ensure the Default Umask is Set Correctly in login.defs
vulnerability.description: To ensure the default umask controlled by /etc/login.defs is set properly, add or correct the UMASK setting in /etc/login.defs to read as follows: UMASK
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q shadow-utils; }; then var_accounts_user_umask='' # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^UMASK") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_accounts_user_umask" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -q -m 1 -i -e "^UMASK\\>" "/etc/login.defs"; then escaped_formatted_output=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$formatted_output") LC_ALL=C sed

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves setting the UMASK value in /etc/login.defs, which controls default file permissions for new users. The remediation is a straightforward configuration change that does not affect authentication, networking, filesystems, or require a reboot. It follows standard STIG hardening patterns and is unlikely to cause system breakage or lockout.

llm_verdict.message:
The password expiration issue is preventing any remediation work. Since I cannot proceed with the UMASK remediation due to this blocking issue, I need to inform the user that the password must be changed first before any system configuration changes can be applied.

The UMASK remediation cannot be completed until the password expiration issue is resolved. The user needs to:

1. Log in interactively to the system
2. Change the expired password using the passwd command
3. Then the UMASK remediation can be applied

I cannot proceed with the OpenSCAP finding remediation until this blocking issue is resolved.
